methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Gravitics is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Colin Doughan that has raised $20M plus $60M in STRATFI financing. The valuation exercise draws from a comparison universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Given data constraints, only the stage-progression method is applicable. Using the $20M total raised as a proxy for pre-money valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $50M. The peer-median approach could not be deployed due to insufficient same- or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace set. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 25.8x across the sector) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies rarely provide.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median of $50M, derived from a single applicable methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of traction metrics (revenue, user count, or technical milestones), which would materially refine stage assessment and multiples. The STRATFI component structure and timing also require clarification to assess its effect on equity dilution and effective post-money valuation. Broader validation would require either additional private-stage comparables or disclosed operational metrics.
